title: IVF & ICSI Treatments - Advanced Fertility Solutions

headings.text: Who Can Benefit from IVF & ICSI?

paragraphs: Advanced treatments tailored to each patient's unique needs, employing evidence-based protocols and state-of-the-art technology.

Eggs are collected from the ovaries and fertilized with sperm in the lab. The process involves retrieving mature eggs from a woman's ovaries and fertilizing them with sperm outside the body, in a laboratory dish.

A single healthy sperm is directly injected into an egg under a microscope, increasing the chances of fertilization. The fertilized eggs, called embryos, are then transferred into the woman's uterus, where they may implant and develop into a pregnancy.
Couples with all normal reports but still unable to conceive
Women with blocked/damaged fallopian tubes or tubal sterilization
Low sperm count, zero sperm count, poor motility, or abnormal morphology
Couples with multiple unsuccessful IUI attempts
Patients with PCOS, endometriosis, or low ovarian reserve
Couples with a history of recurrent abortions
Important Timing: Egg retrieval is performed 34-36 hours after the trigger injection. This timing is critical for successful retrieval of mature eggs.
Controlled ovarian stimulation with down-regulation
Shorter and more flexible for certain patients
Lower dose medications for women with low ovarian reserve
IVF without strong stimulation, using natural ovulation
Risk of twins/triplets (reduced with single embryo transfer)
Ovarian Hyperstimulation Syndrome (rare with modern protocols)
Minimal risks during egg retrieval (bleeding, infection)
Psychological impact of treatment process
Cost of treatment and medications
Not every cycle results in pregnancy
Note: Most side effects are temporary and resolve shortly after treatment completion. Symptoms may include bloating, breast tenderness, mood swings, and mild discomfort.
Success depends on age, egg/sperm quality, and health conditions. Average success rates are 40-50% for women under 35, with reduced chances as age increases.
Note: This is purely dependent on the probability of many variables involved in the treatment.
Our personalized IVF and ICSI treatments are designed to give you the best possible chance of success. Schedule a consultation with our fertility experts today.
content: IVF & ICSI Treatments - Advanced Fertility Solutions IVF & ICSI Treatments Advanced treatments tailored to each patient's unique needs, employing evidence-based protocols and state-of-the-art technology. Schedule Your Consultation What is IVF & ICSI? IVF (In Vitro Fertilization) Eggs are collected from the ovaries and fertilized with sperm in the lab. The process involves retrieving mature eggs from a woman's ovaries and fertilizing them with sperm outside the body, in a laboratory dish. ICSI (Intracytoplasmic Sperm Injection) A single healthy sperm is directly injected into an egg under a microscope, increasing the chances of fertilization. The fertilized eggs, called embryos, are then transferred into the woman's uterus, where they may implant and develop into a pregnancy. Who Can Benefit from IVF & ICSI? Unexplained Infertility Couples with all normal reports but still unable to conceive Tubal Issues Women with blocked/damaged fallopian tubes or tubal sterilization Male Factor Low sperm count, zero sperm count, poor motility, or abnormal morphology IUI Failures Couples with multiple unsuccessful IUI attempts Medical Conditions Patients with PCOS, endometriosis, or low ovarian reserve Recurrent Loss Couples with a history of recurrent abortions IVF & ICSI Treatment Steps Step 1: Initial Assessment & Planning Medical history review and diagnostic tests Creating a personalized treatment plan Step 2: Ovarian Stimulation Baseline ultrasound to check follicle count Daily hormonal injections (FSH/LH) for 8-12 days Medications to prevent premature ovulation Regular monitoring via ultrasounds and blood tests Trigger injection for final egg maturation Step 3: Egg Retrieval Important Timing: Egg retrieval is performed 34-36 hours after the trigger injection. This timing is critical for successful retrieval of mature eggs. Performed under sedation/anesthesia Ultrasound-guided needle aspiration of follicles Goal: 8-15 high-quality mature eggs Minimizing risk of OHSS Step 4: Fertilization IVF: Eggs cultured with sperm in dish ICSI: Single sperm injected directly into egg ICSI increases fertilization chances Step 5: Embryo Culture Embryos cultured for 3-5 days Day 5 embryos called blastocysts (100-250 cells) Fresh transfer or freezing for future use Step 6: Embryo Freezing (Vitrification) High-quality embryos can be frozen for future use Vitrification process prevents ice crystal formation Frozen embryos can be stored for many years Allows for multiple transfer attempts from one retrieval cycle Reduces risk of ovarian hyperstimulation syndrome Step 7: Embryo Transfer Uterus prepared with hormones Painless procedure without sedation Ultrasound-guided catheter placement Usually 1-2 embryos transferred Step 8: Luteal Phase Support Progesterone supplementation Brief rest, avoiding strenuous activity Beta-hCG test 10-14 days after transfer Treatment Protocols Long Protocol Controlled ovarian stimulation with down-regulation Antagonist Protocol Shorter and more flexible for certain patients Mild Stimulation Lower dose medications for women with low ovarian reserve Natural Cycle IVF IVF without strong stimulation, using natural ovulation Risks & Considerations Multiple Pregnancy Risk of twins/triplets (reduced with single embryo transfer) OHSS Ovarian Hyperstimulation Syndrome (rare with modern protocols) Procedure Risks Minimal risks during egg retrieval (bleeding, infection) Emotional Stress Psychological impact of treatment process Financial Considerations Cost of treatment and medications Success Rates Not every cycle results in pregnancy Note: Most side effects are temporary and resolve shortly after treatment completion. Symptoms may include bloating, breast tenderness, mood swings, and mild discomfort. Success Rates Success depends on age, egg/sperm quality, and health conditions. Average success rates are 40-50% for women under 35, with reduced chances as age increases. Note: This is purely dependent on the probability of many variables involved in the treatment. Treatment Preparation Blood investigations and treatment of any medical abnormalities Serology reports and hormonal treatment for ovarian cysts if needed Healthy diet and lifestyle changes Managing weight, stress, and sleep patterns Avoiding smoking and alcohol Taking prenatal vitamins (folic acid, vitamin D, antioxidants) High-protein diet to support egg quality Begin Your Journey to Parenthood Our personalized IVF and ICSI treatments are designed to give you the best possible chance of success. Schedule a consultation with our fertility experts today. Schedule Your Consultation Now
